task_completion.description: OpenRocket application is launched and the original or modified rocket design is loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket GUI window is visible on the screen, showing a rocket design.

task_completion.description: The rocket airframe is modified to include a transition, a 66mm diameter payload bay (>=100mm length), and a 66mm nose cone.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenRocket component tree or 2D/3D view, there should be a visible transition widening the rocket, followed by a new body tube (payload bay) and a nose cone. Component configuration windows may show the 66mm diameter (33mm radius) and >=100mm length.

task_completion.description: An 80g mass component named 'CanSat' is added within the payload bay.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A mass component specifically named 'CanSat' is visible in the component tree, nested under the new payload bay. Its configuration window may show a mass of 80g (0.08 kg).

task_completion.description: The motor is upgraded to a C, D, E, or F class motor, achieving an apogee of at least 50 meters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Motors & Configuration' or 'Flight Simulations' tab, a motor starting with C, D, E, or F is selected. The simulation results table should show an apogee value of 50.0 m or greater.

task_completion.description: The modified design is saved as 'cansat_retrofit.ork'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket window title bar displays 'cansat_retrofit.ork', or a save dialog shows the file being saved to the correct path.

task_completion.description: An integration report is created documenting the payload diameter, mass, selected motor, and simulated apogee.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window (e.g., using 'cat') displays the contents of 'cansat_report.txt', clearly listing the 66mm diameter, 80g mass, the chosen motor, and the simulated apogee.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the rocket design.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the OpenRocket graphical interface (adding components, running simulations), rather than just editing the .ork XML file in a text editor.

integrity.description: The apogee value in the integration report matches the actual simulation results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The apogee documented in the text report should match the apogee shown in the OpenRocket 'Flight Simulations' tab.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply hardcode a fake report without performing the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be evidence of the agent actually selecting a motor and running a simulation before creating the report, rather than just writing a text file with guessed values.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target ORK file: /home/ga/Documents/rockets/cansat_retrofit.ork. Target Report: /home/ga/Documents/exports/cansat_report.txt. Expected payload diameter: 66 mm (radius 33 mm). Original body diameter: 24.8 mm (radius 12.4 mm). Expected CanSat mass: 80 g (0.08 kg). Minimum payload length: 100 mm (0.1 m). Minimum apogee: 50.0 m.
